steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must both explain Kepler's first law (orbits are elliptical) and provide a concrete example that demonstrates the elliptical principle — not orbital period, distance, or anything else.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'cites Umbriel's elliptical orbit as the example': Explains that the law applies to natural satellites and uses Umbriel's elliptical orbit to demonstrate the elliptical principle directly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'provides a generic example': Mentions an orbit but doesn't clarify that it is elliptical — too vague to demonstrate the law's core claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'provides no example': States the law but gives nothing concrete to illustrate it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'uses orbital period as the example': Orbital period is unrelated to orbital shape — the law is about ellipses, not timing. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
